---
title: "From pixels to patterns"
format:
  html:
    css: css/styles.css  
    embed-resources: true
    code-tools: true
    
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

## Packages and data

```{r}
invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))
if (!dir.exists("../output")) {
  dir.create("../output")
}
invisible(lapply(c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "ade4", "piecewiseSEM", "performance", "patchwork", "slider", "dsem", "kableExtra", "phylopath", "e1071", "nlme", "zoo"),require,character.only = TRUE))

position_palette <- c("A" = "#385438", "B" = "#609060", "C" = "#a8c090")
season_palette <- c('autumn' = '#CA7842', "summer" = "#DDA853", 'winter' = '#8ACCD5', "spring" = "#A4B465" )

# Données faune
data_sem_raw <- read.csv("../output/SEM_database.csv", sep = ",", na.strings = c("", "NA"))%>%
  mutate("root" = root_growth_cm2_count,
         "fauna" = all_taxa)
data_sem_raw$date <- ymd_hms(data_sem_raw$date, tz = "UTC")
```

## Effect of land use on soil conditions

### Conditions according to land use

```{r}
#| class: full-width
#| dpi: 72
variable_labels  <- c(hum = "Soil \nhumidity",
        temp = "Soil \ntemperature",
        root = "Root \ndensity",
        fauna = "Invertebrate \nabundance")
position_label <- c(A = "Unmanaged \nsystem",
        C = "Managed \nsystem")

stats <- data_sem_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(date, position, depth, orientation, fauna, root, hum, temp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c('fauna', 'root','hum', 'temp'), names_to = "variable_name") %>%
  mutate(variable_name = factor(variable_name,
                                levels = c("hum", "temp", "root", "fauna")))%>%
  group_by(position, variable_name) %>%
  reframe(
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance = var(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skewness = skewness(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    kurtosis = kurtosis(value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

invisible(tiff("../output/variable_distribution.tiff", width = 3000, height = 1500, res = 300))
variable_distribution_1 <- data_sem_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(date, position, depth, orientation, fauna, root, hum, temp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c('hum', 'temp', 'root','fauna'), names_to = "variable_name") %>%
  mutate(variable_name = factor(variable_name,
                                levels = c("hum", "temp", "root", "fauna")))%>%
  ggplot(aes(value, fill = position)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1, color = 'grey') +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +
  facet_grid(position ~ variable_name, scales = "free",  labeller = labeller(variable_name = variable_labels, position = position_label)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = position_palette) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  labs(y=NULL, x=NULL)+
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(
    data = stats,
    aes(x = Inf - 1, y = Inf,
      label = sprintf("moy=%.2f\nvar=%.2f\nskew=%.2f\nkurt=%.2f",
                      mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis)),
    hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 3, color = "black",
    inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 0, b = 0, l = 10)),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=12, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 0, b = 10, l = 0)),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines"))
variable_distribution_1
invisible(dev.off())

variable_distribution_1
```

### Conditions according to seasons

```{r}
#| class: full-width
#| dpi: 72
variable_labels  <- c(hum = "Soil humidity",
        temp = "Soil temperature",
        root = "Root density",
        fauna = "Invertebrate abundance")
season_levels <- c("winter", "spring", "summer", "autumn")

stats <- data_sem_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(date, season, depth, orientation, fauna, root, hum, temp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c('fauna', 'root','hum', 'temp'), names_to = "variable_name") %>%
  mutate(variable_name = factor(variable_name,
               levels = c("hum", "temp", "root", "fauna")),
         season = factor(season, levels = season_levels)) %>%
  group_by(season, variable_name) %>%
  reframe(
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    variance = var(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skewness = skewness(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    kurtosis = kurtosis(value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

invisible(tiff("../output/variable_distribution_across_season.tiff", width = 2800, height = 1600, res = 300))
variable_distribution_2 <- data_sem_raw %>%
  dplyr::select(date, season, depth, orientation, fauna, root, hum, temp) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c('hum', 'temp', 'root','fauna'), names_to = "variable_name") %>%
  mutate(variable_name = factor(variable_name,
                   levels = c("hum", "temp", "root", "fauna")),
        season = factor(season, levels = season_levels))%>%
  ggplot(aes(value, fill = season)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1, color = 'grey') +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +
  facet_grid(season ~ variable_name, scales = "free",  labeller = labeller(variable_name = variable_labels)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = season_palette) +
  guides(fill = "none") +
  labs(y=NULL, x=NULL)+
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(
    data = stats,
    aes(x = Inf - 1, y = Inf,
      label = sprintf("moy=%.2f\nvar=%.2f\nskew=%.2f\nkurt=%.2f",
                      mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis)),
    hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1, size = 2.5, color = "black",
    inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 0, b = 0, l = 10)),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size=12, margin = margin(t = 10, r = 0, b = 10, l = 0)),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "lines"),  
  panel.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "lines"))
variable_distribution_2
invisible(dev.off())

variable_distribution_2
```

## Effects of land use on the relationships between biotic and abiotic drivers

#### Parameters definition

Definition of the time integration step parameters and maximum allowable missing data rate for calculating the microclimate indices \> **Choice of the daily scale for the moment. But should we use indices? And if so, at what intervals? This choice requires careful consideration ⚠️**

```{r}
time_step_days <- 1
window_size <- time_step_days * 4
missing_data_rate <- 0.3
```

#### Calculation of indices

```{r}
# function to compute indices
vars_to_index <- c("temp", "hum")
index_names <- c("median", "amplitude", "change_ratio", "max", "min")

index_compute_function <- function(vec) {
  if (mean(is.na(vec)) > missing_data_rate) {
    return(c(NA, NA, NA, NA, NA))
  }
  qs <- quantile(vec, c(0.025, 0.05, 0.95, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
  diffs <- diff(round(vec, 6))
  c(
    median       = median(vec, na.rm = TRUE),
    amplitude    = qs[3] - qs[2],
    change_ratio = median(diffs, na.rm = TRUE),
    max          = qs[4],
    min          = qs[1]
  )
}

compute_index_roll <- function(x) {
  res <- rollapplyr(
    x,
    width = window_size,
    FUN = index_compute_function,
    by.column = FALSE,
    fill = NA
  )
  colnames(res) <- index_names
  as_tibble(res)
}

data_sem_indices <- data_sem_raw %>%
  arrange(position, orientation, depth, date) %>%
  group_by(position, orientation, depth) %>%
  mutate(across(
    vars_to_index,
    ~ compute_index_roll(.x),
    .names = "{.col}_index"
  )) %>%
  ungroup()

list_cols <- grep("_index$", names(data_sem_indices), value = TRUE)

for (col in list_cols) {
  data_sem_indices <- data_sem_indices %>%
    unnest_wider(
      !!sym(col),
      names_sep = "_"
    )
}

# index selection
# based on a dimensionality reduction procedure
microclim_data <- data_sem_indices %>% ungroup() %>%
  select(starts_with(c("temp", "hum")))%>%
  drop_na() 

acp_microclimate <- dudi.pca(microclim_data, scannf = FALSE, nf = 2)
#s.corcircle(acp_microclimate$co)
var_coord <- acp_microclimate$co[, 1:2]
var_names <- rownames(var_coord)

microclimate_index <- combn(var_names, 2, function(p) {
  abs(cor(as.numeric(var_coord[p[1], ]), as.numeric(var_coord[p[2], ])))
}) %>%
  { pair_matrix <- combn(var_names, 2); pair_matrix[, which.min(.)] }

cat("Microclimate index selected :", microclimate_index[1], "and", microclimate_index[2])
```

Graphical representation of indices relative to initial variables

```{r}
#| class: full-width
#| dpi: 72

temp_index_1 <- data_sem_indices %>% 
  ggplot(aes(date))+
  geom_line(aes(y= temp), color="#AAC4F5")+
  geom_line(aes(y=temp_index_median))+
  facet_grid(depth+orientation~position)+
  theme_minimal()+
  ggtitle("Median temperature")

hum_index_1 <- data_sem_indices %>% 
  ggplot(aes(date))+
  geom_line(aes(y= hum), color="#C1E59F")+
  geom_line(aes(y=hum_index_median))+
  facet_grid(depth+orientation~position)+
  theme_minimal()+
  ggtitle("Median humidity")

temp_index_2 <- data_sem_indices %>% 
  ggplot(aes(date))+
  geom_line(aes(y= temp), color="#FF937E")+
  geom_line(aes(y=temp_index_amplitude))+
  facet_grid(depth~position)+
  theme_minimal()+
  ggtitle("Daily temperature amplitude")

hum_index_2 <- data_sem_indices %>% filter(orientation=="O")%>%
  ggplot(aes(date))+
  geom_line(aes(y= hum), color="#FCB53B")+
  geom_line(aes(y=hum_index_amplitude))+
  facet_grid(depth~position)+
  theme_minimal()+
  ggtitle("Daily humidity amplitude")

panel <- (temp_index_1 | hum_index_1) / (temp_index_2 | hum_index_2) + plot_annotation(title = "Distribution, over time and space, of calculated indices (in black) relative to initial variables (in color)")

invisible(tiff("../output/variable_indices_distribution.tiff", width = 3000, height = 3000, res = 300))
panel
invisible(dev.off())

panel
```

#### SEM model

```{r}
# Define window size (in days) and allowed missing-data rate
window_days <- 14       
step_days   <- 1      
missing_data_rate <- 0.3


data_sem <- data_sem_indices %>% 
  rename(
    microclimate_index_1 = microclimate_index[1],
    microclimate_index_2 = microclimate_index[2]
  ) %>%
  select(date, position, orientation, depth, fauna, root,
         microclimate_index_1, microclimate_index_2)%>% 
  mutate(depth = as.factor(depth))

# Function to generate rolling windows for a given dataset:
create_rolling_windows <- function(df, window_days, step_days) {
  start_dates <- seq(
    min(df$date),
    max(df$date) - days(window_days) + hours(6),
    by = paste(step_days, "days")
  )
  
  # Subset data for each window
  windows <- map(start_dates, function(start_dt) {
    df %>% filter(date >= start_dt & date < start_dt + days(window_days))
  })
  
  # Name each window by its start date
  names(windows) <- format(start_dates, "%Y-%m-%d")
  windows
}

# Build rolling windows separately for each position:
windows_list <- data_sem %>%
  group_by(position) %>%
  group_map(~ create_rolling_windows(.x, window_days, step_days), .keep = TRUE)
names(windows_list) <- unique(data_sem$position)
```

#### Verification of model fit

```{r}
model_root_A <- lme(root ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2, 
        data = data_sem%>% filter(position=="A") %>% drop_na(), 
        random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth))
model_fauna_A <- lme(fauna ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2 + root,
        data = data_sem%>% filter(position=="A") %>% drop_na(), 
        random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth))

model_root_C <- lme(root ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2, 
        data = data_sem%>% filter(position=="C") %>% drop_na(), 
        random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth))
model_fauna_C <- lme(fauna ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2 + root,
        data = data_sem%>% filter(position=="C") %>% drop_na(), 
        random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth))

cat("performance model *root ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2* for unmanaged system")
check_model(model_root_A)
cat("performance model *fauna ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2* + root for unmanaged system")
check_model(model_fauna_A)
cat("performance model *root ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2* for managed system")
check_model(model_root_C)
cat("performance model *fauna ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2* + root for managed system")
check_model(model_fauna_C)
```

#### SEM application

```{r}
# Function defining the SEM structure 
sem_fit <- function(data) {
  psem(
    lme(root ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2,
        data = data, random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth)),
    lme(fauna ~ microclimate_index_1 + microclimate_index_2 + root,
        data = data, 
        random = ~1 | orientation/depth,
        correlation = corAR1(form = ~1 | orientation/depth)),
    microclimate_index_1 %~~% microclimate_index_2
  )
}

# Storage for fitted models from all rolling windows
results_list <- list()

# Loop over each position and each time window
for(pos in names(windows_list)) {
  for(window_name in names(windows_list[[pos]])) {
    
    data_window <- windows_list[[pos]][[window_name]] %>%
           drop_na()
    
    # Skip windows with insufficient data based on allowed missing rate
    if(nrow(data_window)/(window_days*16) < missing_data_rate) next
    
    # Fit SEM (skip windows that fail to converge)
    mod <- tryCatch(
      sem_fit(data_window),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    
    # Store successful model fits
    if(!is.null(mod)) {
      results_list[[paste(pos, window_name, sep = "_")]] <- list(
        model = mod,
        time_window_start = window_name,
        position = pos
      )
    }
  }
}

# Function to extract coefficients from a fitted SEM:
extract_results <- function(model_name, model_info) {
  sm <- summary(model_info$model)
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(sm$coefficients)
  
  p_clean <- coef_table$P.Value %>%
    as.character() %>%
    stringr::str_replace("<", "") %>%
    as.numeric()
  
  tibble::tibble(
    model            = model_name,
    time_window_start = model_info$time_window_start,
    position         = model_info$position,
    response         = coef_table$Response,
    predictor        = coef_table$Predictor,
    estimate         = coef_table$Estimate,
    se               = coef_table$Std.Error,
    df               = coef_table$DF,
    t_value          = coef_table$t.value,
    p_value          = p_clean
  )
}

# Convert list of model outputs into a single tidy dataframe
results_df <- map2_dfr(
  names(results_list),
  results_list,
  extract_results
)
results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(time_step = as.POSIXct(time_window_start,
                                format = "%Y-%m-%d",
                                tz = "UTC"))
```

##### SEM estimates distribution

```{r}
#| class: full-width
#| dpi: 72
microclimate_index_name  <- c("microclimate_index_1" = microclimate_index[1],
                 "microclimate_index_2" = microclimate_index[2])

g1 <- results_df %>%
  filter(!str_detect(predictor, "~~") & response != "") %>%
  mutate(se = as.numeric(se)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(time_step, estimate, group = position, color = position, fill = position)) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color= "black") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = estimate - se, ymax = estimate + se), alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = position_palette) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = position_palette) +  
  facet_grid(predictor ~ response,scales = "free_y", labeller = labeller(
      response = as_labeller(function(x) paste0("Response: ", x)),
      predictor = as_labeller(microclimate_index_name))) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "2 months") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Estimate", title = "Estimates from SEM regression according to time steps and land use") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        panel.border = element_rect(linewidth =  1))

invisible(tiff("../output/Estimates_SEM_regression_distribution.tiff", width = 3000, height = 2000, res = 300))
g1
invisible(dev.off())
g1
```

##### SEM smoothed estimates distribution

```{r}
# estimates regression
compute_loess <- function(df) {
  fit <- try(
    loess(estimate ~ as.numeric(time_step), data = df, span = 0.5),
    silent = TRUE
  )

df$loess_pred <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(time_step = df$time_step))
  df
}

results_smooth <- results_df %>%
  filter(!str_detect(predictor, "~~") & response != "") %>%
  group_by(position, predictor, response) %>%
  arrange(time_step, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  group_modify(~ compute_loess(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
#| class: full-width
#| dpi: 72
g2 <- ggplot(results_smooth,
       aes(x = time_step, y = loess_pred,
           group = position, color = position, fill = position)) +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = loess_pred), alpha = 0.8, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +

  scale_color_manual(values = position_palette) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = position_palette) +
  facet_grid(
    predictor ~ response,
    #scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(
      response = as_labeller(function(x) paste0("Response: ", x)),
      predictor = as_labeller(microclimate_index_name)
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "2 months") +
  labs(x = "Date", y = "Smoothed estimates (loess)",
       title = "Smoothed estimates from SEM regression according to time steps and land use") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
        panel.border = element_rect(linewidth =  1))


invisible(tiff("../output/Smoothed_estimates_SEM_regression_distribution.tiff", width = 3000, height = 2000, res = 300))
g2
invisible(dev.off())
g2
```
